accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.subtitle: Adjust contrast, text size, and spacing. Saved on this device; if you are signed in, settings also sync to your account.
accessibility.note: Tip: your browser zoom also works, and respects locale ({locale}).

notes.counterIonsOnlyStrongAcids: Counter-ions: only sulfuric adds SO4 and only hydrochloric adds Cl. For other acids these are 0.

salt_additions.v1:
ppm = mg/L

For each salt:
- Convert grams -> moles of salt
- Use stoichiometry to get moles of each ion group
- Convert to mg of each ion group
- Divide by volume (L) to get ppm

Final ions = base ions + sum(per-salt deltas)

acidification.v1:
We solve for an acid amount that reaches a target pH given alkalinity and volume.

Key relationships (simplified):
- final_alkalinity = effective_alkalinity - acid_required(mEq/L) × 50
- acid_required depends on carbonate distribution + acid dissociation at target pH

Counter-ions:
- sulfuric adds SO4
- hydrochloric adds Cl

mash_overall.v1:
Overall ions = ions(after salts) + acid counter-ions, and HCO3 is derived from final alkalinity.

This combines:
- salt additions (mass-balance on ppm)
- acidification (final alkalinity + SO4/Cl counter-ions)

sparge_overall.v1: Overall ions = ions(after salts) + acid counter-ions, and HCO3 is derived from final alkalinity.

boil_overall.v1: Overall ions = ions(after salts) + acid counter-ions, and HCO3 is derived from final alkalinity.

pre_boil_volume.v1: pre_boil_volume = mash_water + sparge_water - grain_absorption - mash_losses - leftover + boil_additions

og.v1: OG is estimated from fermentables yield, efficiency (%), and kettle volume.

pbg.v1: PBG is estimated from fermentables yield, efficiency (%), and pre-boil volume.

attenuation.v1: Effective attenuation = average of the top 2 yeast attenuations (overrides win).

ibu_tinseth.v1: Tinseth IBU is computed from hop additions, utilization, boil gravity, and post-boil volume.

ibu_rager.v1: Rager IBU is computed from hop additions, utilization, boil gravity, and post-boil volume.

mcu.v1: MCU (Malt Color Units) = Σ(lb × °L) / gal, using post-boil/kettle volume.

common.toggleHint: Applies to all sections on this page (where available).
common.excludeDryhop: dry hop does not contribute to IBU
common.noteDependsOnWaterAndEquipment: Depends on saved water settings + equipment snapshot.
common.noteMissingWaterSettings: Missing saved water settings; volume estimates may be unavailable.
common.noteMissingFermentableColors: Missing fermentable color (Lovibond) on one or more grist rows.

abv.title: ABV (estimated)
abv.body:
Formula:
ABV = (OG - FG) × 131.25

This recipe:
OG = {og}
FG = {fg}
ABV = {abv}%

Notes:
- OG/FG are derived estimates (not measured).

ibuTinseth.title: IBU (Tinseth, estimated)
ibuTinseth.body:
Model (Tinseth):
U = 1.65 × 0.000125^(G - 1) × (1 - e^(-0.04 × t)) / 4.15
IBU = Σ (g × (AA%/100) × U × 1000 / V_L)

This recipe:
Gravity used (SG) = {gravity} ({gravitySource})
Volume used (L) = {volume} ({volumeSource})

Hop additions (from editor):
{hopsLines}

Result:
IBU (Tinseth) = {ibu}

ibuRager.title: IBU (Rager, estimated)
ibuRager.body:
Model (Rager):
U% ≈ 18.11 + 13.86 × tanh((t - 31.32) / 18.27)
Gravity adjustment (if G > 1.050): GA = (G - 1.050) / 0.2
IBU = Σ (g × (AA%/100) × (U%/(1+GA)) × 1000 / V_L)

This recipe:
Gravity used (SG) = {gravity} ({gravitySource})
Volume used (L) = {volume} ({volumeSource})

Hop additions (from editor):
{hopsLines}

Result:
IBU (Rager) = {ibu}

srmMorey.title: Color (SRM, Morey, estimated)
srmMorey.body:
Model (Morey):
MCU = Σ (lb × °L) / gal
SRM = 1.4922 × MCU^0.6859

This recipe:
Kettle volume used = {volume} L
Notes:
- {notes}

Result:
SRM (Morey) = {srm}

srmDaniels.title: Color (SRM, Daniels, estimated)
srmDaniels.body:
Model (Daniels, linear):
MCU = Σ (lb × °L) / gal
SRM ≈ 0.2 × MCU + 8.4

This recipe:
Kettle volume used = {volume} L
Notes:
- {notes}

Result:
SRM (Daniels) = {srm}

kettleVolume.title: Volume (kettle)
kettleVolume.body:
This value is derived from your saved water settings and equipment losses/evaporation.

This recipe:
Kettle volume = {kettleVolume} L

Notes:
- {notes}

preBoilVolume.title: Pre-boil volume (estimated)
preBoilVolume.body:
This value is derived from your saved water settings and equipment losses.

This recipe:
Pre-boil volume = {preBoilVolume} L

Notes:
- {notes}

og.title: OG (estimated)
og.body:
OG is estimated from fermentables potential + volume + efficiency.

This recipe:
OG = {og}
Volume (kettle) = {volume} L
Efficiency used = {efficiency}%

Notes:
- If efficiency/fermentables are missing, OG can be unavailable.

fg.title: FG (estimated)

attenuation.title: Attenuation (effective)
attenuation.body:
Model:
- For each yeast: use override% if present, else use BeerJSON attenuation (avg(min,max) if given).
- Effective attenuation = average of the top 2 highest effective values.

This recipe:
All yeasts:
{yeastLines}

Selected (top 2):
{selectedLines}

Top-2 average = {topAvg}%
Effective attenuation = {attenuation}%

pbg.title: PBG (pre-boil gravity, estimated)
pbg.body:
PBG is estimated like OG, but using the pre-boil volume.

This recipe:
PBG = {pbg}
Pre-boil volume = {preBoilVolume} L
Efficiency used = {efficiency}%

Notes:
- Requires saved water volumes to estimate pre-boil volume.

acidRequired.title: Acid required (target pH)
acidRequired.body:
Conceptual model:
- We solve for an acid amount that brings Starting pH -> Target pH, given Starting alkalinity and water volume.

Notes:
- If the selected profile has no pH, we only use the manually entered Starting pH.
- Acid type/strength changes the conversion from equivalents to mL/grams.
- This is a heuristic calculator: use it as guidance, then measure/adjust.

finalAlkalinity.title: Final alkalinity (ppm as CaCO3)
finalAlkalinity.body:
Meaning:
- Alkalinity after the calculated acid addition.

Notes:
- It can be near 0 when acid neutralizes the buffering capacity.
- We report alkalinity in ppm as CaCO3 (standard brewing convention).
finalAlkalinity.bodyWithValues:
Meaning:
- Alkalinity after the calculated acid addition.

This recipe:
Starting alkalinity = {startingAlk} ppm as CaCO3
Final alkalinity = {finalAlk} ppm as CaCO3

Notes:
- It can be near 0 when acid neutralizes the buffering capacity.
- We report alkalinity in ppm as CaCO3 (standard brewing convention).

ionsAfterSalts.title: Resulting ions (after salts only)
ionsAfterSalts.body:
Meaning:
- Starting from the selected sparge profile, we add ions contributed by the entered salt grams at the given sparge water volume.

Notes:
- Values are ppm (mg/L) for each ion.
ionsAfterSalts.bodyWithValues:
Meaning:
- Starting from the selected sparge profile, we add ions contributed by the entered salt grams at the given sparge water volume.

This recipe:
Volume = {volumeL} L

Salts:
{saltsLines}

Ions (ppm):
{ionsLines}

Notes:
- Values are ppm (mg/L) for each ion.

ionsAfterSaltsAndAcid.title: Resulting ions (after salts + acid)
ionsAfterSaltsAndAcid.body:
Meaning:
- Same as “after salts”, then apply the acid result.

Notes:
- SO4/Cl increases come from the acid counter-ion model (when applicable).
- HCO3 is derived from alkalinity (proxy), not measured bicarbonate.
ionsAfterSaltsAndAcid.bodyWithValues:
Meaning:
- Same as “after salts”, then apply the acid result.

This recipe:
Volume = {volumeL} L
Final alkalinity = {finalAlk} ppm as CaCO3

Salts:
{saltsLines}

Ions (ppm):
{ionsLines}

Notes:
- SO4/Cl increases come from the acid counter-ion model (when applicable).
- HCO3 is derived from alkalinity (proxy), not measured bicarbonate.

alkalinityHeuristic.title: Heuristic: Ca/Mg reduce effective alkalinity
alkalinityHeuristic.body:
We apply a residual-alkalinity-style adjustment:
- Calcium and magnesium from salts modestly reduce effective alkalinity, so acid required can change slightly when salts are added.

Notes:
- This is intentionally modest; it improves realism without overfitting.

acidRequired.title: Acid required (target pH)
acidRequired.body:
Conceptual model:
- We solve for an acid amount that brings Starting pH -> Target pH, given Starting alkalinity and mash water volume.

Notes:
- Acid type/strength changes the conversion from equivalents to mL/grams.
- This is a heuristic calculator: use it as guidance, then measure/adjust.
acidRequired.bodyWithValues:
Conceptual model:
- We solve for an acid amount that brings Starting pH -> Target pH, given Starting alkalinity and mash water volume.

This recipe:
Starting pH = {startingPh}
Target pH = {targetPh}
Starting alkalinity = {startingAlk} ppm as CaCO3
Mash water volume = {volumeL} L

Result:
{detailsLines}

Notes:
- Acid type/strength changes the conversion from equivalents to mL/grams.
- This is a heuristic calculator: use it as guidance, then measure/adjust.

finalAlkalinity.title: Final alkalinity (ppm as CaCO3)
finalAlkalinity.body:
Meaning:
- Alkalinity after the calculated acid addition.

Notes:
- It can be near 0 when acid neutralizes the buffering capacity.
- We report alkalinity in ppm as CaCO3 (standard brewing convention).
finalAlkalinity.bodyWithValues:
Meaning:
- Alkalinity after the calculated acid addition.

This recipe:
Starting alkalinity = {startingAlk} ppm as CaCO3
Final alkalinity = {finalAlk} ppm as CaCO3

Notes:
- It can be near 0 when acid neutralizes the buffering capacity.
- We report alkalinity in ppm as CaCO3 (standard brewing convention).

ionsAfterSalts.title: Resulting ions (after salts only)
ionsAfterSalts.body:
Meaning:
- Starting from the mixed source water, we add ions contributed by the entered salt grams.

Notes:
- Values are ppm (mg/L) for each ion.
- This table does not include acid; see the overall result for combined output.
ionsAfterSalts.bodyWithValues:
Meaning:
- Starting from the mixed source water, we add ions contributed by the entered salt grams.

This recipe:
Mash water volume = {volumeL} L

Salts:
{saltsLines}

Ions (ppm):
{ionsLines}

Notes:
- Values are ppm (mg/L) for each ion.
- This table does not include acid; see the overall result for combined output.

overallSnapshot.title: Overall mash snapshot (salts + acid)
overallSnapshot.body:
Meaning:
- Combined result after applying both salts and acid.

Notes:
- SO4/Cl increases come from the acid counter-ion model (when applicable).
- HCO3 is derived from alkalinity (proxy), not measured bicarbonate.
overallSnapshot.bodyWithValues:
Meaning:
- Combined result after applying both salts and acid.

This recipe:
pH = {ph}
Mash water volume = {volumeL} L
Final alkalinity = {finalAlk} ppm as CaCO3

Ions (ppm):
{ionsLines}

Notes:
- SO4/Cl increases come from the acid counter-ion model (when applicable).
- HCO3 is derived from alkalinity (proxy), not measured bicarbonate.

ionsAfterSalts.title: Resulting ions (after salts only)
ionsAfterSalts.body:
Meaning:
- Starting from the mixed source water, we add ions contributed by the entered salt grams.

Notes:
- Values are ppm (mg/L) for each ion.
ionsAfterSalts.bodyWithValues:
Meaning:
- Starting from the mixed source water, we add ions contributed by the entered salt grams.

This recipe:
Boil water volume = {volumeL} L

Salts:
{saltsLines}

Ions (ppm):
{ionsLines}

Notes:
- Values are ppm (mg/L) for each ion.

overallSnapshot.title: Overall boil snapshot (salts + acid)
overallSnapshot.body:
Meaning:
- Combined result after applying both salts and acid.

Notes:
- SO4/Cl increases come from the acid counter-ion model (when applicable).
- HCO3 is derived from alkalinity (proxy), not measured bicarbonate.
overallSnapshot.bodyWithValues:
Meaning:
- Combined result after applying both salts and acid.

This recipe:

Final alkalinity = {finalAlk} ppm as CaCO3

Ions (ppm):
{ionsLines}

- SO4/Cl increases come from the acid counter-ion model (when applicable).
- HCO3 is derived from alkalinity (proxy), not measured bicarbonate.

mergedWaterRecap.title: Merged water recap
mergedWaterRecap.body:
Meaning:
- This section summarizes each stream (mash/sparge/additional boil) and the merged summary.

Notes:
- Merged pH is an approximate volume-weighted [H+] mix (rule-of-thumb).
- Streams without saved snapshots are excluded from merged ions.

mergedIons.title: Merged ions (ppm)
mergedIons.body:
Meaning:
- Computed from saved mash/sparge/boil snapshots after salts + acid (SO4/Cl counter-ions only) and averaged by volume.

Notes:
- Streams without saved snapshots are excluded.
- HCO3 is derived from alkalinity (proxy), not measured bicarbonate.
mergedIons.bodyWithValues:
Meaning:
- Computed from saved mash/sparge/boil snapshots after salts + acid (SO4/Cl counter-ions only) and averaged by volume.

This recipe:
Ions ({ppm}):
{ionsLines}

Notes:
- Streams without saved snapshots are excluded.
- HCO3 is derived from alkalinity (proxy), not measured bicarbonate.

recipes.title: Recipes
recipes.subtitle: This is the recipe area entrypoint (list/edit/water calculator).

import.title: Import recipe
import.subtitle: Import a recipe file and create a new recipe in your account.
import.singleSubtitle: Import one recipe file into your account (you choose the style).
import.unitsNote: Units: metric and US customary are accepted. UK imperial gallons are not supported yet.
import.customImportNote: Need UK imperial gallons or another system import?
import.customImportCta: Contact us for affordable custom import.
import.bulkSubtitle: Import a file containing multiple recipes and create them all at once.
import.formatNotResolved: Unable to detect format from filename. Choose a format above.
import.bulkStyleRule: Bulk import matches style to BJCP 2021 by exact name first, then code. If no match, we assign Custom.

errors.unknownFormat: Unknown format. Please choose BeerJSON or BeerXML.
errors.previewMissing: Preview response is missing expected data.
errors.importMissingId: Import response is missing recipe id.
errors.fileTooLarge: File too large. Maximum size is {max}.
errors.fileTooLargeHelp: If you're experiencing this error, try splitting the file into smaller parts. Paid tier users can contact our support for assistance.

edit.title: Edit recipe
edit.shapeNote: Single-page editor with section navigation. Water chemistry is a link-out to the dedicated calculator page.
edit.notReadyToLoad: Not ready to load this recipe.
edit.mashingHelp: Mash schedule (BeerJSON mash procedure). Import a BeerJSON/BeerXML recipe with mash steps, or add steps manually in the Mash and/or Sparge water calculator pages.
edit.mashingEmpty: No mash steps. Add steps or import a recipe with a mash schedule.
edit.mashingEditInMashPage: Edit mash schedule in Mash water & Mash steps
edit.mashingWaterVolumesUnavailable: Set up water volumes in the Water calculator to see mash and sparge volumes here.
edit.mashStepsWaterBudgetNote: Total water across all mash steps cannot exceed the 'Mash [total] water volume' above. Only steps with 'Deduce from Mash in' checked deduct from the Mash water volume. This is useful for brewers who use hot water for temperature increase.
edit.mashStepsMashInAlwaysPresentNote: The "Mash in" step is always present by design: it guarantees total quantity correctness. If you need an immediate successive step (i.e. 'Protein rest') use the "Add step" button and add it. You can have a new step with 0 amount.
edit.mashStepsTypeFallbackNote: If you do not find the step mash you're looking for in the 'Type' list use 'Temperature' and add the name (like 'Protein rest' or 'Ferulic rest') in the 'Name' field.
edit.mashStepsSeeRecapLink: See Mash recap in recipe summary here.
edit.mashingSpargeStepAmountUnavailable: Set up water volumes in Water calculator

equipmentSection.help: Choose an account-level equipment profile to snapshot into this recipe (no live reference).

edit.hopsHelp: Pick hops from the database (or enter manually). Stored as a snapshot on the recipe.
edit.yeastHelp: Select yeast from the database (or enter manually). Stored as a snapshot on the recipe.
edit.waterHelp: The full mash chemistry + water calculator lives on its own page (not embedded here).
edit.rawMaterialsCtaPrefix: Found a missing or incorrect raw material?
edit.rawMaterialsCtaLinkText: Help improve the raw material database

analysis.help: Derived estimates. If required inputs are missing, values show as “Insufficient data”.

warnings.missing_beerjson: Recipe is missing BeerJSON; cannot compute analysis.
warnings.missing_water_settings: Missing saved water settings; volume estimates may be unavailable.
warnings.missing_water_volumes: Missing mash/sparge water volumes; volume estimates may be unavailable.
warnings.invalid_runoff_volume: Mash/sparge volumes and losses imply zero or negative runoff volume; check water + mash equipment inputs.
warnings.invalid_evaporation: Boil evaporation rate/time imply zero or negative post-boil volume; check equipment inputs.
warnings.invalid_kettle_volume: Computed kettle volume is zero or negative; check equipment losses and water inputs.
warnings.exceeds_kettle_capacity: Computed kettle volume exceeds kettle capacity/target from equipment profile.
warnings.missing_efficiency: Missing efficiency; OG/PBG estimates require an efficiency %.
warnings.missing_fermentables: No fermentables with usable amount + yield/potential; cannot estimate OG/PBG.
warnings.missing_color_volume: Missing computed kettle volume; SRM requires saved water settings + volumes.
warnings.missing_fermentable_colors: Missing fermentable color (Lovibond) on one or more grist rows; SRM requires fermentable colors.
warnings.used_batch_size_volume: Using BeerJSON batch_size volume for IBU estimate because computed kettle volume is unavailable.
warnings.missing_ibu_gravity: Missing boil gravity estimate; IBU estimates require PBG/OG.
warnings.missing_ibu_inputs: No usable hop boil/whirlpool additions with amount, alpha acid %, and time; cannot estimate IBU.
warnings.missing_attenuation: Missing yeast attenuation; FG/ABV estimates require yeast attenuation or an override.

mash.title: Mash water & Mash steps
mash.adjustmentHint: Select a source + dilution profile and set volumes to see mixed ions.
mash.gristSummaryHelp: We avoid duplicating the full grist table here. Use the recipe editor for details; this page only keeps a snapshot for calculations.
mash.lateExtractExcludedNote: Late extract (kettle) additions are excluded from mash grist for water chemistry.
mash.overallResultHeading: Overall mash water result (HCO3 derived from alkalinity)

sparge.title: Sparge water
sparge.saltAdditionsHelp: Base profile is the selected sparge water profile above. Add salts in grams; we compute resulting ions (ppm) for the sparge water volume.

boil.title: Additional boil water
boil.overallResultHeading: Overall boil water result (HCO3 derived from alkalinity)
boil.adjustmentHelp: Choose source/target/dilution profiles and volumes to compute a mixed starting water profile.
boil.saltAdditionsHelp: Select a Source profile and set Source volume. Dilution is optional, but if Dilution volume is > 0 you must select a Dilution profile.
boil.saltAdditionsBaseHelp: Base profile is the mixed source water above. Add salts in grams; we compute resulting ions (ppm).

delete.confirmBody: This cannot be undone. Click “Delete now” to confirm, or Cancel to keep it.

export.strictNote: Exports are strict BeerJSON (internal addition row ids are removed for interoperability).

waterProfiles.title: Water profiles
waterProfiles.createdProfilesStartUnverified: Created profiles start as unverified. Use the table actions to verify/unverify.
waterProfiles.rawMaterialsCtaPrefix: Found a missing or incorrect raw material?
waterProfiles.rawMaterialsCtaLinkText: Help improve the raw material database

contributing.title: Contributing
contributing.subtitle: Help improve translations and brewing datasets used by the app.

rawMaterials.title: Help improve raw materials database
rawMaterials.subtitle: If you find missing or incorrect fermentables/hops/yeast/other ingredients (or water-related profiles), tell us so we can improve the canonical dataset.
rawMaterials.step1: Note what’s missing/incorrect (name, producer, key specs like color/yield/AA% where applicable).
rawMaterials.step2: Include a screenshot or the exact values if possible (helps avoid ambiguity).
rawMaterials.step3: Open a report using our Raw materials issue template (coming soon).
rawMaterials.issueTemplateNote: We’ll add a direct link once the GitHub contribution workflow is finalized.

waterHub.title: Water management
waterHub.missingHeaders: Missing dev headers. Go to the dashboard and click Save headers (User + Active account), then come back here.
waterHub.recapSubtitle: Uses saved snapshots where available; merged pH is an approximation using volume-weighted [H+].
waterHub.mergedIonsDescription: Merged ions (ppm) are computed from saved mash/sparge/boil snapshots after salts + acid (SO4/Cl counter-ions only) and averaged by volume. Streams without saved snapshots are excluded.
waterHub.noMergedProfile: No merged profile available yet (need saved salts + acid snapshots for at least one stream).
waterHub.finalRecapSubtitle: A quick, heuristic summary (not a full water chemistry model).
waterHub.finalRecapCaveat: Caveat: RA is a heuristic; results depend on assumptions and do not replace a full mash pH/chemistry model.
waterHub.styleExpectedRaPale: Pale / hop-forward styles usually benefit from lower RA.
waterHub.styleExpectedRaAmber: Amber / malty styles often tolerate moderate RA.
waterHub.styleExpectedRaDark: Dark / roasty styles often benefit from higher RA.
waterHub.alkVsBicarbSubtitle: Why this app uses CaCO3 for alkalinity/RA and HCO3 in the ion tables.
waterHub.alkVsBicarbPoint1: “ppm as CaCO3” is an alkalinity unit: it expresses acid-neutralizing capacity (not dissolved CaCO3).
waterHub.alkVsBicarbPoint2: Ion tables list species concentrations (ppm): Ca, Mg, Na, SO4, Cl and HCO3.
waterHub.alkVsBicarbPoint3: When the table says “HCO3 derived from alkalinity”, it converts CaCO3-equivalent alkalinity into an HCO3 ppm estimate: HCO3 ≈ alkalinity × 61/50.
waterHub.alkVsBicarbPoint4: Strictly, alkalinity and bicarbonate are not identical at all pH/conditions; this conversion is a practical approximation for typical brewing water.

about.title: About
about.subtitle: Brewery App is an internal tool for recipe development and brew-day logging, with a water chemistry calculator inspired by BrunWater, Troester and others. Beer.json schema fully supported. Raw material database of choice for v0 is BeerProto.
about.translationsRowPrefix: Translations are welcome and managed via our contribution workflow (see
about.translationsSideNote: Side note: this is just a primer — the translation contribution workflow is planned, but not fully in place yet.

selectAccount.title: Select account
selectAccount.subtitle: Choose which brewery/account you want to work in.

auth.noteBody: English is the original language. Other languages (starting with Italian) will soon be 100% supported. Spanish and German are coming soon as well.

i18nContributing.title: Help translate (i18n contributing)
i18nContributing.subtitle: We welcome translation contributions. English is the source of truth; Italian is the first community language.
i18nContributing.howItWorks1: The web app uses locale-prefixed URLs (e.g. /en/..., /it/...).
i18nContributing.howItWorks3: Keys should be stable. Don’t change keys unless you’re intentionally refactoring text across the app.
i18nContributing.recommendedToolBody: We recommend using Weblate (web UI) connected to GitHub. It allows anyone to translate without touching git, and it opens PRs automatically.
i18nContributing.recommendedTool1: Translate using a browser (no git required).
i18nContributing.recommendedTool2: Maintainers review and merge Weblate PRs.
i18nContributing.githubFallbackBody: If you’re comfortable with git/GitHub, you can also open a PR editing the JSON message files directly.
i18nContributing.rule2: Keep meaning accurate; avoid overly literal translations if they read unnaturally.
i18nContributing.rule3: Don’t translate technical identifiers like file paths, API paths, or code.
